evals.prompt: Analyze the public page https://example.com/ in style-only mode and turn its visual system into a systematic prompt and reusable Codex Skill. Do not copy branding or source text. Save the complete package to the requested output directory.
evals.expected_output: A validated StyleSpec, 15-section systematic prompt, evidence report, tokens, and generated style Skill with URL facts separated from inference.
expectations: The run creates non-empty style-spec.json, systematic-prompt.md, evidence-report.md, variables.css, and generated-style-skill/SKILL.md files.
style-spec.json validates against the UItoPrompt 1.0 contract and uses metadata.rightsMode style-only.
systematic-prompt.md contains all 15 canonical sections in order.
The output distinguishes browser or page facts from inference and does not claim unsupported responsive, interaction, or motion facts.
The generated package excludes source branding and source copy and includes explicit negative constraints.

evals.prompt: I only have the supplied desktop screenshot. Extract the UI style for Claude Code, save a complete prompt package, and be honest about what cannot be known from one image.
evals.expected_output: An image-evidence package whose typography family, DOM, breakpoints, hidden states, and motion remain explicit unknowns.
expectations: The run creates non-empty style-spec.json, systematic-prompt.md, evidence-report.md, variables.css, and generated-style-skill/SKILL.md files.
The output measures image dimensions or palette instead of presenting every visual claim as model inference.
Exact font family, DOM semantics, source breakpoints, hidden interaction states, and motion are explicit unknowns or authored fallbacks rather than claimed facts.
systematic-prompt.md contains all 15 canonical sections in order.
The output validates its StyleSpec before handoff and reports any remaining evidence boundary.

evals.prompt: Treat the supplied product screenshot as a general visual reference rather than a page to clone. Translate its editorial mood, rhythm, color roles, and geometry into a reusable web-app visual style package. Keep the principles, not the artwork or copy.
evals.expected_output: Translated visual principles and UI tokens marked as translated, plus rights-safe negative constraints and acceptance targets.
expectations: At least one StyleSpec section is marked translated and cites visual-reference evidence.
The package converts composition, contrast, rhythm, texture, and shape into executable UI rules without claiming source DOM, breakpoints, or interaction facts.
The output explicitly excludes the reference artwork, copy, branding, and protected assets.
systematic-prompt.md contains all 15 canonical sections in order.
The generated style Skill is reusable beyond the supplied screenshot and explains how to handle unknowns.
